metadata.claim_text: Students often underweight exam findings and over-rely on imaging when evaluating multiligament knee injury.
Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for multiligament knee injury — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.
State mechanism, key exam findings, imaging role, treatment concept, and return-to-play framework for multiligament knee injury.
Rehabilitation milestones and return-to-play criteria for multiligament knee injury require attending-defined progression gates.
Knee dislocation and multiligament injury require documented distal perfusion and ABI when indicated.

metadata.action: Discuss operative reconstruction versus continued rehabilitation; graft selection and fixation are attending decisions
metadata.trigger: Active athlete with functional instability or mechanical symptoms from multiligament knee injury despite appropriate initial management

metadata.action: Apply sport-specific return-to-play criteria with strength, stability, and functional testing milestones
metadata.trigger: Athlete approaching return-to-sport after multiligament knee injury treatment

metadata.claim_text: Multiligament knee injuries demand vascular exam, reduction, and staged reconstruction planning.
Multiligament Knee Injury pathoanatomy centers on acl, pcl, mcl and related stabilizers.

metadata.action: Emergent vascular evaluation and revascularization before definitive ligament reconstruction
metadata.trigger: Multiligament knee injury with absent or diminished distal pulses

metadata.description: Characteristic MRI findings in multiligament knee injury.
More mobile lateral meniscus with popliteus hiatus; commonly torn with ACL injuries.
Distal biceps insertion on the radial tuberosity enabling elbow flexion and supination.
Lateral ankle ligament stabilizing subtalar motion after inversion injury.
Superior labrum and biceps anchor complex at the glenoid origin.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Multiligament Knee Injury Complication

metadata.description: Common post-injury complication in multiligament knee injury.
Posterior cuff tendon providing external rotation strength.
Lateral elbow stabilizer resisting varus stress.
Hyaline cartilage covering femoral condyles and tibial plateau.
Medial elbow stabilizer critical in throwing athletes.
Primary lateral knee stabilizer resisting varus stress.
Cross-neighborhood reference to canonical condition tibial-plateau-fracture.
Cross-neighborhood reference to canonical condition acl-tear.
Conjoined gastrocnemius-soleus tendon inserting on the calcaneus.
Superior rotator cuff tendon initiating abduction and commonly torn in cuff pathology.
